what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard ledger item that defines a template for capturing a user submission: an intent category, blob-referenced attachments, and a body containing a comment or voice transcript.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-5f3284ed, address board://item-5f3284ed, type block, source claude_code, state current, title 'P11 · --- intent + attachments ---'
The item belongs to channel dragnet-development and links part_of board://item-e42d651f
The markdown body is a structured template with fields: intent (change-request | question | idea | bug), attachments ([blob://<algo>:<hash>, …]), and body ('<Tim's comment text / voice transcript>')
The intent values are described as 'registry-ref'd' and the body placeholder names a specific person, Tim
No actual attachments, hashes, or filled body are present; it is a schema/example rather than a populated record
standouts: The file is explicitly a template/schema for 'intent + attachments' rather than a concrete record
It references an external parent item via part_of link to board://item-e42d651f
It mixes structured metadata (YAML) with a human-readable template body that includes a voice-transcript placeholder
conventions: Follows the ledger item format with YAML frontmatter and a markdown body
Uses board:// URI scheme for ledger addresses and blob:// URI scheme for attachment references
Uses registry-referenced categorical tags and placeholder comments inside angle brackets
concerns: The body is only a placeholder with no actual content, so the item's real payload is missing
The attachments field is illustrative syntax with no real blob hashes or metadata
The 'registry-ref'd' intent values are not accompanied by a registry link or identifier
The thread field is empty and the relationship to item-e42d651f is unexplained
notes: A noticeboard template item (P11) that sketches a structured submission format for intent, attachments, and a transcript body, linked as part of item-e42d651f.
questions: What registry defines the allowed intent values?
Who is Tim and is the body placeholder meant to be replaced per instance?
What is item-e42d651f and what role does this item play within it?
Are attachments required, and what blob algorithm/hash format is expected?
Is this file a one-time example or a reusable template to be copied?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose ('intent + attachments') matches the file's content, though the file is a template/schema rather than a populated item.
apparent_themes: Attachment and media support in ledger items
Minimal templating over a ledger substrate
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a reusable noticeboard item format for recording user requests or observations, complete with intent classification, supporting attachments, and verbatim text, so the system can route or process them consistently.
novelty: It combines registry-referenced intent tags, blob-store attachment references, and a voice-transcript body placeholder into a single lightweight noticeboard template, naming a specific person in the placeholder.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, reusable schema for user-submitted intent items with attachments and body text, which a fused system could keep as a reference input format.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item is a template for a structured ledger block that captures an intent category, blob-referenced attachments, and a comment or voice-transcript body.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to standardize how user requests, questions, ideas, or bugs are recorded with supporting attachments and verbatim text.
